summary: Website redesign and China Going-Global report are the two biggest initiatives in motion, with Intelligent China program launch and social media agency selection urgent this week.
summaryDetail: The team is juggling two major projects: the knowledge website redesign, where Joker's and Joseph's designs are being merged with a decision to keep menu categorization and disperse videos by topic, and the 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' report co-published with IE Business School, targeting a mid-September launch in Singapore. Urgent this week: launching the Intelligent China program webpage, expediting the social media agency selection (with Media Minds favored), and finalizing the Asia Start campaign's dual-track approach. Upcoming: the Tech in Asia partnership must be confirmed by end of July, and the team needs to analyze participant profiles for Asia Star and Intelligent China programs, which are prioritized over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, noting it's similar to current site with main change being menu categorization. Discussed incorporating ideas from Joseph's more editorial-style design to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing programs in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, school brochure updates, CES quotes, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency search. Concerns raised about Zhu Yang's ChatGPT-generated feedback and timeline for agency selection.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses co-publishing a report on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with a main research piece from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools. They plan to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start Campaign & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping cooperation.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep knowledge menu centered (preferred over left-aligned).
Show more articles on homepage, possibly with pinned article and latest section.
Bring BCI and other indices to knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star (November) and Intelligent China (December) programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; content optimization can begin immediately.
Menu categorization by topics is correct; keep professor analysis highlighted.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu is needed, not two.
Zhu Yang's feedback is likely ChatGPT-generated; limit time spent on it.
Media Minds may be the best agency choice given budget constraints and timeline.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report will include main research by Professor Leeway, op-eds from both schools, and industry case studies.
Target launch at Tech in Asia (Sep 15-16) or Tyson event (Sep 29-30) in Singapore.
Digital version ready by early September for promotion through end of year.
Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month.
decisions.meetingTitle: Asia Start Campaign & New Program Planning

decisions.text: Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start to avoid cannibalization.
decisions.meetingTitle: Asia Start Campaign & New Program Planning

decisions.text: CKGSB X Tech In Asia partnership needs to be confirmed within July to allow time for planning and recruitment.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: 答复: CKGSB X Tech In ASIA

decisions.text: Global Marcom's report final version approved and sent to Shouhuo.
Social media ad agency bidding will evaluate three agencies: AdsMarch, Media Minds Global, and 微盟营销.
